markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' decision to delay Grand Theft Auto VI from its May 26, 2026 target to November 19 has extended the development window by six months, allowing extra time for polish on the Vice City-set open-world title. This shift, confirmed in Take-Two's earnings updates, widens the runway for cultural milestones, tech releases, and industry events to unfold beforehand while marketing is expected to intensify through the summer. Traders monitor signals like upcoming quarterly calls and any new trailers for momentum shifts, as the prolonged wait heightens uncertainty around what could resolve first in this high-profile entertainment cycle.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent developments in Harvey Weinstein’s legal proceedings, particularly the May 15 mistrial in his third New York rape retrial on the Jessica Mann charge, have shaped trader sentiment around future incarceration. With the Manhattan district attorney weighing a potential fourth trial and Weinstein, now 74 and in declining health, already serving time from his 2025 New York criminal sexual act conviction and 16-year California sentence, markets reflect expectations of limited additional prison time. Ongoing appeals in both jurisdictions, possible plea negotiations, and the cumulative effect of years already served at Rikers Island contribute to the 62.6% implied probability on no further incarceration. Upcoming sentencing on the New York count and any appeal outcomes remain key swing factors for resolution.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Carolina Hurricanes hold the edge in trader consensus for the 2026 Stanley Cup title, driven by their dominant postseason run that includes just one loss across multiple series and a regular-season mark of 53-22-7. Vegas advanced by sweeping the top-seeded Avalanche with elite defensive structure and timely scoring, while benefiting from extra rest ahead of the June 2 opener in Raleigh. Carolina’s home-ice advantage, depth across forward lines featuring players like Sebastian Aho and Seth Jarvis, and overall consistency position them as the clear favorite in this best-of-seven matchup. The Golden Knights’ top-end talent and shutdown potential keep their implied probability competitive, but recent form and situational factors favor the Eastern Conference champions.

eventMetadata.context_description: The San Antonio Spurs hold the edge in 2026 NBA Championship odds thanks to Victor Wembanyama’s dominance and their grueling path through the Western Conference, including a Game 7 win over the Oklahoma City Thunder that showcased superior depth and health. The Knicks, riding an 11-game postseason winning streak with blowout victories in the East behind Jalen Brunson, enter as underdogs but benefit from rest and proven playoff momentum. Trader consensus reflects the Spurs’ regular-season consistency and matchup advantages against New York, though the closely contested series leaves room for adjustments based on injury updates or early Finals results.

markets.question: Will the San Antonio Spurs win the 2026 NBA Finals?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the San Antonio Spurs win the 2026 NBA Finals. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NBA Finals based off the rules of the NBA.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NBA.

markets.question: Will MegaETH perform an airdrop by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MegaETH launches a token and performs an airdrop by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market "locked" tokens or non-swappable tokens will not suffice to resolve this market to "Yes". Airdrops of NFTs will not qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market is on-chain information and official information from MegaETH, however a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

events.title: MegaETH airdrop by...?
events.description: this is a market on MegaETH airdrop

markets.question: Will Spain win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the national team that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: France, Spain, and England lead the market as the top-ranked sides by FIFA standings and recent international results, with Portugal, Brazil, and Argentina close behind due to deep attacking options and historical pedigree. Multiple high-profile injury concerns, including hamstring issues for Spain’s Lamine Yamal and foot problems for Mikel Merino, plus absences for France’s Hugo Ekitike and Brazil’s Rodrygo, have introduced uncertainty that narrows the gap among these contenders. Pre-tournament squad finalization and group-stage draws further balance probabilities, while the expanded 48-team format increases the chance of early upsets or deep runs by mid-tier European and South American sides. The tight clustering reflects the wisdom of crowds assessing comparable squad quality and form across the leading nations just weeks before kickoff.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping’s entrenched position within the Chinese Communist Party, marked by the absence of any designated successor and the 2018 removal of presidential term limits, drives the strong trader consensus that he will remain in office through the 21st Party Congress in 2027. Analyses from early 2026 indicate preparations for a fourth term, with elite ranks filled by his allies and no visible factional challenges or institutional pressure for an earlier exit. Military anti-corruption efforts have proceeded without signs of broader instability or leadership realignment. While low-probability disruptions from health developments or sudden elite shifts remain possible, current conditions show no credible catalysts for removal before the end of 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gavin Newsom holds the leading position among potential 2028 Democratic presidential nominees in a fragmented field, reflecting his term-limited status as California governor, sustained national visibility through policy clashes with the current administration, and early donor and media outreach. Recent polling shows varying support across hypothetical matchups, with governors and senators such as Josh Shapiro, Pete Buttigieg, and Andy Beshear appealing to moderates while Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez draws progressive backing amid her national tour and undecided stance. Kamala Harris retains residual name recognition from the prior cycle. Key factors that could consolidate support include 2026 midterm outcomes, early-state polling trends, fundraising totals, and endorsements ahead of primaries, with no candidate yet formally entering the race.
